request.question: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for 4 weeks will reduce intestinal permeability by 30% vs controls, measured by FITC-dextran assay, due to upregulation of claudin-1 and occludin.

hypothesis.intervention: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for 4 weeks
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific. No missing details.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG C57BL/6 mice intestinal permeability FITC-dextran claudin-1 occludin
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Supplementing C57BL/6 mice with Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for 4 weeks will reduce intestinal permeability by 30% vs controls, measured by FITC-dextran assay, due to upregulation of claudin-1 and occludin.

references.title: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG culture supernatant ameliorates acute alcohol-induced intestinal permeability and liver injury.
references.relevance_note: This paper directly discusses Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG and its effects on intestinal permeability, which aligns closely with the hypothesis.

references.title: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG prevents epithelial barrier dysfunction induced by interferon-gamma and fecal supernatants from irritable bowel syndrome patients in human intestinal enteroids and colonoids
references.relevance_note: Though this study is in human models rather than mice, it addresses similar themes of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG and intestinal barrier function, supporting the relevance of the hypothesis.

literature_qc.explanation: The retrieved paper [3] examines Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG, specifically how it prevents epithelial barrier dysfunction in an in vitro model. The biological system (C57BL/6 mice) is similar to the one proposed in the hypothesis, focusing on the functional outcome of improving intestinal permeability, a measurable outcome aligning with the proposed FITC-dextran assay method. Since the intervention and biological system are closely related, this indicates an prior study directly relevant to the current hypothesis.
literature_qc.recommended_action: A paper matching this experiment was found in the scientific literature. Review it before proceeding. If this is intentional replication, state that explicitly. If not, differentiate your approach.

risks.description: Control strategy may miss process-specific artifacts in handling or reagent preparation.

plan.objective: To determine the effects of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG supplementation on intestinal permeability in C57BL/6 mice.
plan.experimental_design: This study will involve dividing C57BL/6 mice into two groups: a Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG treatment group and a control group. Mice in the treatment group will receive the probiotic supplement for four weeks, after which intestinal permeability will be assessed using the FITC-dextran assay.
controls: C57BL/6 mice administered with 20 mL of sterile saline as a negative control.
C57BL/6 mice receiving 10^9 CFU/mL of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG in sterile saline solution.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 2 h | Animal Preparation
Obtain 20 male C57BL/6 mice (6-8 weeks old) from Jackson Laboratory, barcoded for identification.
Expected outcome: 20 C57BL/6 mice are available for treatment.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 7 days | Animal Preparation
Acclimate mice in separate cages under standard conditions (22±2°C, humidity 40-60%, 12:12 light/dark cycle) for 1 week prior to experiment.
Expected outcome: Mice exhibit adapted behavior after 1 week.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 24 h | Probiotic Preparation
Culture Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG in MRS broth at 37°C for 24 h until reaching an OD600 of 1.0 (approximately 10^9 CFU/mL).
Expected outcome: Culture reaches 10^9 CFU/mL as determined by OD600 measurement.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Probiotic Preparation
Centrifuge the culture at 4,000 × g for 10 min at 4°C to pellet the bacteria.
Expected outcome: Bacterial pellet obtained at the bottom of the centrifuge tube.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 10 min | Probiotic Preparation
Resuspend the pellet in 20 mL of sterile saline.
Expected outcome: Bacterial suspension ready for administration.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Treatment Administration
Randomly assign mice to two groups (n=10 per group): treatment (Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG) and control (saline) using random number generator.
Expected outcome: Mice are randomly assigned to experimental groups.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Treatment Administration
Administer 0.5 mL of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG suspension (10^9 CFU/mL) to the treatment group by oral gavage each day for 4 weeks.
Expected outcome: All treatment group mice receive the probiotic without complications.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Treatment Administration
Administer 0.5 mL of sterile saline to the control group by oral gavage.
Expected outcome: All control group mice receive saline without complications.

DAY 7 | 1 h | Monitoring
Monitor mice for health and weight weekly, recording any signs of distress or health issues.
Expected outcome: Weekly health data collected for all mice.

DAY 14 | 1 h | Monitoring
Continue health monitoring and weight assessments weekly; adjust feed as necessary.
Expected outcome: Consistent health checks performed, adjustments made as needed.

DAY 21 | 30 min | FITC-dextran Preparation and Administration
Dilute 4000 mg of FITC-dextran (4 kDa, Sigma-Aldrich) in 100 mL of sterile saline to a final concentration of 40 mg/mL.
Expected outcome: FITC-dextran solution prepared at specified concentration.

DAY 21 | 30 min | FITC-dextran Administration
Administer 0.6 mL of 40 mg/mL FITC-dextran solution by oral gavage to all mice.
Expected outcome: All mice receive FITC-dextran without complications.

DAY 21 | 45 min | Sample Collection
After 4 h of FITC-dextran administration, euthanize mice using isoflurane anesthesia followed by cervical dislocation.
Expected outcome: All mice are humanely euthanized and ready for tissue collection.

DAY 21 | 1 h | Tissue Collection
Collect intestinal tissue samples from all mice for analysis of permeability markers.
Expected outcome: Tissue samples collected from all mice for further analysis.

DAY 21 | 30 min | FITC Analysis
Prepare tissue samples by homogenizing in 1 mL of PBS and centrifuge at 3,000 × g for 10 min.
Expected outcome: Tissue samples homogenized and centrifuged for supernatant extraction.

DAY 21 | 1 h | FITC-dextran Measurement
Measure FITC-dextran levels in the supernatant using a fluorometer (BioTek Synergy 4) at an excitation wavelength of 485 nm and emission wavelength of 528 nm.
Expected outcome: FITC-dextran concentrations quantified for each sample.

DAY 21 | 2 h | Statistical Analysis
Perform t-tests to compare intestinal permeability measured by FITC-dextran levels between treatment and control groups, setting significance level at p<0.05.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined for differences in permeability.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG will survive and colonize the intestine of C57BL/6 mice after oral administration.
FITC-dextran accurately reflects intestinal permeability in the experimental model.
The intervention period of 4 weeks is sufficient to observe changes in intestinal permeability.
decision_criteria: Proceed if intestinal permeability is reduced by at least 30% in treatment group compared to the control group.
Confirm successful administration of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG before analysis.
Verify that all mice show normal health indicators prior to sacrifice.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG for all administrations.
Maintain consistent diet and housing conditions for all mice throughout the experiment.
Calibrate all instruments (weighing scale, fluorometer, etc.) prior to the study.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: 30% reduction vs controls improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To determine the effects of Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG supplementation on intestinal permeability in C57BL/6 mice.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
cro_ready_brief.materials_responsibility: CRO to quote and confirm all materials before ordering.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing logs in each run?
Can you include replicate-level batch metadata in the final report?
